title: approvals — broadcast channels
description: Real-time broadcast channels for approval-lifecycle events. Three channels serve three audiences: tenant-wide feed for admin dashboards, per-user feed for requester + approver inboxes, per-instance feed for detail-view live updates.

channels.name: tenant.{tenantId}.approvals
channels.description: Tenant-wide approval-lifecycle feed. Every event on any instance in the tenant broadcasts here so admin dashboards can live-update the list of open + recently-decided approvals. Batched — a burst of decisions in bulk-approve coalesces to one broadcast per second per instance.
channels.authorize: User belongs to the tenant AND has `approvals.instances.viewAny`

channels.name: user.{userId}.approvals
channels.description: Per-user approval feed. Requesters subscribe to see status updates on their own instances; approvers subscribe to see new incoming approvals they need to review. Auth: caller IS the user.
events: ApprovalRequested (for approvers only — filtered on payload.resolved_approvers)
ApprovalExpired (for the requester + any pending approvers)

channels.name: approval-instance.{instanceId}
channels.description: Per-instance feed. Powers the approval-detail live view — when a decision is made on one requirement, everyone with the detail page open sees the timeline update without polling. Auth: caller can view the specific instance (requester, current-or-past approver, tenant admin).

notes: The `approval-instance.{instanceId}` channel replaces detail-page polling — when an approver is watching a request they submitted, decisions land in the timeline in real-time.

ApprovalSecurityViolation is deliberately NOT broadcast. It writes to the audit log + fires ApprovalExecutionFailedNotification to platform-ops but never surfaces on tenant channels — the tenant should not learn from a real-time feed that a forgery attempt was made against them.

ApprovalExecutionFailed fires on `tenant.{tenantId}.approvals` only when the executing action's namespace is tenant-visible (billing.*, access.*). Platform-internal action failures (identity.*.internal) stay on platform-ops channels.
